description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and two prominent side outlets for hose connections. The hydrant features a central front-facing outlet with a cap, flanked by two side outlets, each secured with a metal chain to prevent loss of the caps. The surface is painted a bright, slightly weathered red, showing signs of wear and some darkened areas from use or exposure. The top of the hydrant has a hexagonal operating nut, used to open the valve for water flow. The base is wide and circular, designed for stability when bolted to the ground, and the overall construction appears robust, likely made of cast iron or steel.
description: A robust, red, cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped outlets and metal chains, showing signs of wear and weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central outlet with a cap and chain, flanked by two side outlets and the domed top with the operating nut.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting one side outlet with its cap and chain, and partial visibility of the front outlet.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant with both side outlets visible, but no central outlet or operating nut in view.
description_view_3: Right-side view showing the opposite side outlet with its cap and chain, and partial visibility of the front outlet.
